POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:113] In fact the currently used method also makes the created requirement greedy:
https://github.com/eclipse-equinox/p2/blob/5b57f58f9ad96c332dcf99218b24dbc5a5cfb705/bundles/org.eclipse.equinox.p2.metadata/src/org/eclipse/equinox/p2/metadata/MetadataFactory.java#L543C39-L543C43

So this is already as desired.
v4:
SUMMARY:
PR #4771 makes `jars.extra.classpath` platform URL entries optional by changing the optional flag from `false` to `true` in `createRequirementFromPlatformURL`, aligning Tycho with PDE's behavior where unresolvable entries (e.g., platform-specific SWT bundles on incompatible OS) are silently ignored instead of causing resolution failures.

POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:112] The core fix is correct. Changing the optional flag to `true` means p2 will not fail resolution when a referenced bundle (e.g., `org.eclipse.swt.cocoa.macosx.aarch64`) cannot be found on an incompatible environment. This is consistent with existing tycho patterns: `FeatureDependenciesAction.java:121-123` respects `entry.isOptional()` for feature entries, and `BundlesAction.java:347-348` creates optional requirements for source bundles.

- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:40-43] The import reordering (moving `OptionalResolutionAction` above `StatusTool` and `TychoConstants`) is cosmetic alphabetical sorting. Harmless but unnecessary — the only functional change is line 112.

- **Missing test coverage (recommendation):** No existing test covers the scenario where a `jars.extra.classpath` entry is unresolvable. The TYCHO253 test (`tycho-its/src/test/java/org/eclipse/tycho/test/TYCHO253extraClassPathEntries/ExtraClassPathEntriesTest.java`) references `org.apache.ant` and `com.jcraft.jsch`, which are available on `http://download.eclipse.org/releases/latest`. Consider adding an integration test with a deliberately unresolvable platform-specific entry (e.g., a macOS-only bundle ID) to verify that the build succeeds rather than fails with a dependency resolution error. This would prevent regression and document the expected behavior.

- **Scope is appropriately narrow:** `createRequirementFromPlatformURL` is private and called only from `createRequirementFromExtraClasspathProperty` within the same class, so the change affects only `jars.extra.classpath` and `extra.<jar>=` entries from `build.properties`. Other requirement creation paths (Require-Bundle, features, products) are unaffected. The `PLATFORM_URL_PATTERN` regex (`platform:/(plugin|fragment)/([^/]*)(/)*.*`) correctly captures the bundle ID from platform URLs, and only matched URLs are converted to requirements.
v5:
SUMMARY:
The PR changes the `optional` flag from `false` to `true` in `MetadataFactory.createRequirement()` for `jars.extra.classpath` entries, making unresolvable platform-specific dependencies (e.g., fragments) silently ignorable — aligning with PDE behavior.

POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:113] The change correctly targets the 5th parameter (`optional`) of `MetadataFactory.createRequirement(String, String, VersionRange, String, boolean optional, boolean multiple)`. Verified against `BundleDependenciesAction.java:162-168` which documents the same parameter ordering with explicit comments. The switch from `false` to `true` makes extra classpath entries optional, so unresolvable platform-specific bundles (e.g., `org.eclipse.swt.cocoa.macosx.aarch64` on Linux) no longer cause resolution failures.
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:87-104] Note that `OptionalResolutionAction` (REQUIRE/IGNORE/OPTIONAL) is passed to `getPublisherActions()` but NOT to `getPublisherAdvice()`, where `getExtraEntriesAdvice()` is invoked. Hardcoding `optional=true` here is intentional (to match PDE), but it means extra classpath entries are always optional regardless of any `OptionalResolutionAction` configuration — this is a design choice worth acknowledging.
- No existing test covers the unresolvable-optional-entry scenario. Consider adding an integration test (e.g., a fragment with `jars.extra.classpath` pointing to platform-specific bundles that cannot resolve in the current environment) to validate the fix and prevent regression. The existing test `ExtraClasspathEntriesTest.java` only covers resolvable entries.
- The diff also includes a cosmetic import reorder (`OptionalResolutionAction` and `StatusTool` swapped to alphabetical order). No functional impact.
